Title: Improving 10-deacetylbaccatin III-10-b-Oacetyltransferase catalytic fitness for Taxol production

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro enzyme system for Taxol production
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus bark/tissues; in this study product formed in vitro
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer drug; also used for psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis and restenosis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically hydrolyses 7-β-xylosyl-10-deacetyltaxol (XDT) to 10-deacetyltaxol (DT), enabling downstream Taxol production in the one-pot system
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LXYL-P1-2 was the specific β-xylosidase paired with engineered DBAT in the one-pot system. 2. It converted XDT to DT efficiently under the reaction conditions used for the coupled process. 3. Combined with DBATG38R/F301V, it enabled production of ~0.64 mg ml-1 Taxol in 50 ml at 15 h.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: One-pot coupled enzyme assay; HPLC product quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro reaction at 37.5 °C, pH 5.5, 2 mM XDT, 0.5 mg ml-1 enzyme chosen for coupling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic optimum differs from standalone optimum; coupled system operated under DBAT-optimal rather than LXYL-P1-2-optimal conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biocatalyst for environmentally friendly Taxol production from abundant XDT; suitable for enzyme coupling and possible synthetic biology deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic efficiency on XDT was much higher than DBAT mutant efficiency on DT, so DBAT remained the bottleneck in the coupled system

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyl-CoA-dependent acyltransferase superfamily; conserved HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro enzyme system for Taxol production
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late taxane acetylation step at C10; natural route from 10-DAB to baccatin III and engineered conversion from DT to Taxol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Baccatin III from 10-DAB; Taxol from DT
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to the C10 hydroxyl of 10-DAB or DT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late-pathway control step for conversion of 10-DAB to baccatin III; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms baccatin III in the native pathway and directly forms Taxol from DT in the engineered route
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus bark/tissues; in this study product formed in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer drug; also used for psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis and restenosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type DBAT from T. cuspidata acetylates DT to Taxol, but with much lower catalytic efficiency than on natural substrate 10-DAB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant T. cuspidata DBAT was validated to acetylate DT into Taxol by HPLC-MS and NMR. 2. Activity on DT was nearly three orders of magnitude lower than on 10-DAB. 3. Structural modeling and mutagenesis identified active-site and substrate-channel residues affecting catalytic fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; HPLC-MS; NMR; alanine scanning; saturation mutagenesis; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays; optimum 40 °C for 10-DAB, 37.5 °C for DT; optimum pH 5.5
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Improving activity toward DT can reduce activity toward natural substrate 10-DAB depending on mutation; acetyl-CoA supply limits scale-up
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central engineering target for biocatalytic or synthetic biology Taxol production from XDT/DT; amenable to protein engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: His162 acts as catalytic base; residues Ile164, Phe44, Gly359, Gly361, Arg363 and Phe400 contribute to binding/catalysis; Phe301 influences substrate specificity switch

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro enzyme system for Taxol production
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered Taxol production from XDT via DT
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group from acetyl-CoA to the C10 hydroxyl of DT to form Taxol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck-improved engineered step in the coupled XDT-to-Taxol system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer drug; also used for psoriasis, rheumatoid arthritis and restenosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Double mutant markedly improves DT acetylation and enables efficient one-pot conversion of XDT to Taxol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Combining G38R and F301V created a synergistic DBAT mutant with 3.7-fold higher specific activity on DT than wild type. 2. The mutant reached kcat/Km 3.539 M-1 s-1, about sixfold higher than wild type. 3. Together with LXYL-P1-2, it produced ~0.64 mg ml-1 Taxol in 50 ml after 15 h.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; one-pot bioconversion with HPLC and structural confirmation of product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro reaction at 37.5 °C and pH 5.5; 1.5 mg ml-1 enzyme with 3 h supplementation in coupled reaction
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activity toward natural substrate 10-DAB decreased to ~69% of wild type; enzyme stability/activity loss required supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineered biocatalyst for green Taxol manufacturing; useful starting point for further activity/stability optimization and microbial pathway construction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: G38R likely improves substrate entry via increased surface hydrophilicity; F301V reduces steric hindrance in solvent channel for bulky DT

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates DT at C10 using acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Improved catalytic variant, not final best mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves DBAT activity on DT to 218.77% of wild type while also increasing activity on 10-DAB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Saturation mutagenesis at Gly38 identified G38R as a superior variant. 2. G38R increased DT activity ~2.19-fold over wild type. 3. This mutation was incorporated into the best double mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Saturation mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay at DBAT optimal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: None obvious for 10-DAB activity; not as effective as double mutant on DT
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mutation for stacking in DBAT engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely favors entry of both DT and 10-DAB by increasing local hydrophilicity near enzyme surface

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates DT at C10 using acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Improved catalytic variant, not final best mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves DBAT activity on DT to 285% of wild type but reduces activity on natural substrate 10-DAB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Saturation mutagenesis at Phe301 identified F301V as the best single mutant at this site. 2. F301V increased DT activity ~2.85-fold over wild type. 3. The mutation likely enlarges the solvent channel for bulky DT but compromises 10-DAB catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Saturation mutagenesis; enzyme activity assay; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay at DBAT optimal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 10-DAB activity decreased to ~52.6% of wild type
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful specificity-switch mutation for engineering DBAT toward bulky non-natural substrate DT
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports a role of Phe301 in acyl-acceptor substrate specificity switching by steric hindrance
